entry.title: SAP Social Sabbatical – Reflections & Gratitude
entry.content:
Just before SAP Sapphire in Orlando this year, I returned from my SAP Social Sabbatical program in Kenya. I am sharing with you my take on this incredible program because I realize that many are not familiar with it, yet the program presents an amazing opportunity for all SAP catalysts and executives to drive positive change - both within our communities and for our personal and professional growth.

SAP Social Sabbatical is an award-winning portfolio of global volunteering consultancy programs that empower SAP employees to share their knowledge, skills, and expertise with non-profits and social enterprises all around the world. In its purest form, the social enterprise is a real, income-generating business, but one that has a clear social mission. It is a pioneering and burgeoning sector, numbering in the millions worldwide.
SAP is not a social enterprise, but we are inspired by them and our Social Sabbatical program is an opportunity to engage with these inspiring ventures and their visionary founders. It is also an opportunity to experience SAP's purpose - to help the world run better and improve people’s lives - on a very tacit and personal level.

To date, the program has positively impacted 7M+ lives, helped create 460+ jobs, and supported 531 organizations worldwide through 1,601 SAP employees, of which 47% have been our women colleagues. Within it, there are various global, regional, and local programs tailored for SAP executives and the talent in the Catalyst Development Program, so everyone can find an opportunity to volunteer for something suited to their skillset

Personally, I had stepped away from my day-to-day responsibilities for two weeks to participate in the Kenya program, but I came back with years of energy and positivity to give back to our business, our team, SAP and the communities we serve. That amount of motivation, drive and purpose, is the tangible outcome of our Social Sabbatical program I would like all of you to experience.

I met very young people who already know their potential, and who are incredibly capable. They are executing very strategic world-class programs and leveraging technology and AI as much as some of the most capable in our teams in SAP today. But, while globalization and technology have made education more accessible to all over the last couple of decades, they couldn’t offer access to equal local and global opportunities at the same pace. SAP stepped up to help through the best we have to offer – access to markets, our technology and our incredible and skilled talent.

I supported a social enterprise called Taka Taka Ni Mali, founded by one of Kenya’s most successful women entrepreneurs who was named the Woman Entrepreneur of the Year in Kenya in 2018. The name of her enterprise in Swahili means ‘Waste is Wealth’ as the business focuses on managing and recycling waste, with a vision to turn Kenya into an environmentally sustainable nation. The business relies on donors to fund their enterprise, but Mary, the founder, is intent on making Taka Taka Ni Mali a financially independent enterprise with sustainable long-term success.

We were tasked with helping the business explore monetization opportunities to self-fund its social mission. Two weeks later, two of my SAP colleagues and I, representing three different SAP Board areas, redesigned Taka Taka Ni Mali’s entire business model, identifying five key monetization opportunities, and building the financial model and business plan for two of them. The plan was adopted, and the execution started while we were still in Nairobi!
These two weeks had a massive impact on my personal growth too.

Being taken out of my comfort zone - from the daily hum and drum of the business world - and being thrown into a problem that affected the lives of the people in front of me, brought a new level of clarity, efficiency and personal motivation to resolve that problem! The importance of finding a personal connection between the problem and my motivation to solve it, is one of the key lessons from this experience for me.

I was also successfully reminded about the power of conviction – seeing the determination and the absence of fear to go after one’s conviction even in such challenging environments, reignited my personal conviction in the journey we have charted for our Digital motion. I come back stoked, knowing that a few determined individuals can indeed move mountains. We, on the other hand, are a +1,000 men and woman-strong team in the Customer Success Digital motion – our goals need to remain incredibly ambitious - because we CAN attain them.

SAP is setting an incredibly high bar in the industry with this program, and I encourage you all to check if you are eligible to participate at least once in your career. Programs like these help us align our actions with the values we stand for and the culture we strive to uphold - where empathy and kindness can be, and are, executed in harmony with our grit and high performance.

I’ve always been proud to be an SAPer, but I’ve never been as proud as I am now!

SAP Social Sabbatical program - KenyaTaka Taka Ni MaliTaka Taka Ni Mali

entry.title: Report on SAP Signavio Process Intelligence data using the new OData API and SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Intro

With our August 2024 release, we are happy to share the general availability of our OData API to extract analytics data from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

SAP Signavio’s OData API is a scalable, out-of-the-box interface. It aims to make process mining data available to a broader audience and accessible in 3rd party tools other than SAP Signavio, including e.g. SAP SAP Analytics Cloud and Microsoft Excel. 

Users can integrate valuable process insights in their existing enterprise dashboards, democratizing the value of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence overall. 

Additionally, SAP’s data integration capabilities like SAP Cloud Integration within SAP Business Technology Platform can be leveraged to further ease the integration of the OData API. 

This will play a crucial part for our customers to streamline their process mining insights into central analytics and reporting tools, such as SAP Analytics or related BI solutions.

In this blog post, we would like to give you an overview of this new capability.

We will guide you through the steps on how to create the API token, then how to setup an OData view that defines which data can get extracted by the API and then ending with the actual consumption of the API in Microsoft Excel and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Create API Token

Let's start by selecting the process.

In the top right corner, select the process settings option.

When you now enter your process settings within a process of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, you will find a new tab called "OData Views". Here you can define an API token that is required for the technical authentication against the API and also an OData view that selects the right data from your event log.

Let's start by creating a new API token. Here you can define the new name of the token and set the validity duration of the token. By default, the duration is set to 90 days.

Once you done, you can simply create your token, which then displays your token exactly once. Hence, make sure to copy it down as you will not be able to retrieve the token again once you leave the page.

Create OData View

Now we want to define the data that can be extracted with this OData API. For that we require the same SIGNAL query we use on our dashboards to query the data in our event log. In this example, I used a table widget on a dashboard and viewed it in SIGNAL mode, where I can easily copy over the SIGNAL query into my clipboard.

Now going back to my OData view tab where I created my API token beforehand, I can create an OData view.

Here I can provide a new name for the OData view, a description relating the data that can be extracted and also paste in the SIGNAL query which I copied over from the table widget in my dashboard.

Note that the column name in your SIGNAL query can't have a blank space, hence we recommend you to replace it with an underscore or letter.

Once you are done, you can click on create and you get navigated back to the OData view page, where your newly created OData view is ready to be used.

Load data into Microsoft Excel

To perform a quick test against the API before connecting it to proper enterprise applications, you can use tools such as Postman or Microsoft Excel. In this example, as it is an OData API, you can simply open a new Excel worksheet and get data via the power query option.

Here you select the option OData.

Here you need to define the URL and insert the API token as password under basic authentication. Note that the URL is built up in this format:

https://<baseUrl>/pi/signal/odata/v1/<resource>

The base URL is based on the API endpoint where your Signavio workspace is hosted. In my case the name of the OData view is "NPS_Case_Status".  The username is not important as the API token is implicitly already linked to your Signavio user. In this case, I used "unknown", but you can use any username as it will not be applied. More details on this can be found in our official documentation.

Once the API call was successful, you should see the dataset of your OData API.

Once you are happy with the data query, you can close and load the data into your excel worksheet.

Connect API to SAP Analytics Cloud

Now we want to use the API to extract data into SAP Analytics Cloud. For that we can create connection.

Here we select the OData Services option.

Here we can provide the OData Connection a name, URL and the API token, similarily to the setup in the Excel worksheet. Also here, the user name is set as mandatory, but will not be technically required for the authentication against the API. In our example, we use a Signavio workspace in the EU region, which can be seen in the respective URL, which can be found in the documentation. Again here, the user name is not required. I used "not_required", but you can use any word as it doesn't get used during authentication.

More details on this can be found in our official documentation.

Once the connection was successfully saved, we can now used it in a model.

You can use your newly created connection as a baseline for your model.

Then the system displays you all created OData Views where your user has access to. In my example, I have access to these four OData views that were able in my single process. We can select the "NPS_Case_Status" OData API and provide it a similar name.

After that we can select what columns we want to display in SAP Analytics Cloud. In our case, we would like to see all data as part of the OData API.

Once the data was successfully imported, you should be able to the newly extracted data under data foundation. Here you can use the extensive analytical capabilities in SAP Analytics Cloud to merge this model with your existing analytics artifacts.

And that is it! Let me know what you think of this new API and what you use it for. In case of questions, feel free to reach out to us.

Additional resources

SAP Help

SAP Business Accelerator Hub

August 2024 release summary

Postman Collection

Enable Now

Analytics API in SAP Signavio Process Governance

entry.title: Setup of the SAP Signavio Process Insights connector within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content:
Great news!

With the new plug and gain connector the process of refreshing data is becoming smoother and more focused on customer needs: from now on, customers who have completed the initial set-up, up can individually take-over the “plug”-phase at any desired time, which strongly minimizes involvement of external efforts.

The SAP Signavio plug and gain is a comprehensive program designed to facilitate the rapid adoption and value realization of the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite for SAP ERP customers who want to move into the Cloud. The suite offers a set of tools aimed at improving business process management and optimization.

 While the initial  set-up of plug and gain is still delivered with support from SAP, the dependency on SAP support to actively use plug and gain on a regular basis is not required anymore. Instead, the customer has all means and abilities to conduct a data refresh whenever required, taking into account cost management with focus on available records. This drives the integration of plug and gain into our SAP Signavio suite, making it an integral part with streamlined release cycles. It is important to note that existing PnG customers cannot run the connector against their old plug and gain, it must set it up again.

Come again? What is plug and gain by SAP Signavio?

In summary, the plug and gain approach is an accelerated approach to process and ERP transformation. The plug and gain approach allows you to combine the capabilities of SAP Signavio Process Insights to measure process performance and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to learn about actual process execution and define your to-be processes. On top of this, you are equipped with a variety of analyses to improve processes, your current ERP system and a possible target landscape.

As part of the plug and gain approach a connection between SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA, or SAP Ariba to SAP Signavio Process Insights is established. Then, the data is loaded from SAP Signavio Process Insights to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence quickly and easily. Typical use cases for implementing plug and gain include the following:

  • To help reduce the complexity and cost of SAP S/4HANA transformation projects
  • To address challenges arising from mergers and acquisitions, resulting in distributed system landscapes and limited process harmonization
  • To handle the challenge of ERP systems that have grown historically with custom development, and obsolete configuration settings and data
  • To avoid costly process mining projects that require a lot of setup time before processes can be improved

In case you want to learn more about leveraging the plug and gain approach yet, don’t worry. You can easily catch up here. Make sure to contact your SAP counterpart to get started with plug and gain by SAP Signavio.

How to set up the connector?

  • Navigate via “Process Data Management” to “Connections” and click “Create” to establish the Process Insights connection. Here, as seen in the screenshot, name the connection. Then select “SAP Signavio” as the connection type to create the connection.

Provide the requested details in the credentials inside the connection. The following hints will help you fill out the form correctly:

  • OAuth Cliend ID: the Client ID of the service key from your Process Insights API instance is required here
  • OAuth Client Secret: the Client Secret of the service key from your Process Insights API instance is required here
  • Service Root: the service link including the ending to define which service should be called
  • The service root always starts with “https://bpi-plug-and-gain.cfapps.eu11.hana.ondemand.com/api/”  and the selected service you want to connect to has to be appended to this URL.
  • List of available Services:
    • Process Landscape Analysis: EventLogsService/v1 
    • Invoice to Cash: Finance_I2CService/v1
    • Invoice to Pay: Finance_I2PService/v1
    • Acquire to Decomission: AssetManagementService/v1
    • Source to Pay and Source to Pay incl. Ariba: SourcingAndProcurementService/v1
    • Lead to Cash: SalesService/v1
    • Plan to Fulfill: ManufacturingService/v1
    • Record to Report: RecordToReportService/v1
  • You can specify in the Service Root which service to call, so which data to be extracted from Process Insights using the connector. E.g.: URL for Source to Pay data: https://bpi-plug-and-gain.cfapps.eu11.hana.ondemand.com/api/SourcingAndProcurementService/v1/
  • OAuth Access Token URL: this URL is specific to your Process Insights Tenant URL. In general, it looks like this: https:// <YourProcessInsightsTenant>.authentication.<YourServerRegion>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
  • Target Currency: Please select the main currency that you want to use in the content.

 Setting up the Process Data Pipeline:

  • You now have to access the following note and download the content that is attached. You can access the collective note over this link: https://me.sap.com/notes/3496399/E and within this note, you can select the underlying note that contains the content you want to set up
  • From here, import the Process Data Pipeline by clicking on “Import” in the “Process Data Pipelines” menu, and drag-and-drop the .etl file into the pop-up to upload the Process Data Pipeline.
  • Once the Process Data Pipeline is uploaded, the source data will be imported as well, containing all the relevant tables for the set up.

Adding the connector to the pipeline

  • Add a process to store your dashboards once the pipeline is set up: Click on “New Process” or reuse an existing process, provide a name, and create the process.

Data Extraction

  • Before you can trigger the first data extraction over the pipeline, please make some optional adjustments to the source data tables within the source data.
  • By clicking on one of the tables on the arrow on the right side, you are provided with more options under “Configurations” to configure which data should be extracted.

  • You also can adjust the SQL-Filter here, where you can put in “collectionNumbers = 5”, meaning that for this table, five data collections within Process Insights will be loaded. Per standard, this will be pre-configured to “1”, meaning that by default, one data collection gets loaded.
  • You can also specify the systems within a Process Insights tenant. This is relevant for you, if you have multiple systems connected, but you only want to extract data from one certain system. This can be done by inserting system = “Name of system” into the SQL-Filter.
  • Once you have made all the adjustments in the tables, you can start the initial data extraction. You can trigger this by going into the pipeline and trigger the “Run ETL” option on the top-right in the pipeline overview.

  • Once you triggered the ETL, the extraction should begin.
  • Once the data has been extracted, the ETL overview should look like the below screenshot and the status should be “completed”:
  • At this point, we have extracted the data from Process Insights for the first time. Now we want to set up the content on top of the extracted data.
  • Process Setup
    • To get started with your analysis, you now need to import the content that we provide with plug and gain. First, please import the BPMN Models and example diagrams for the Value Accelerators into your Signavio Process Manager. 
    • Please navigate into the Process Manager, and click “Import SAP Signavio archive (SGX)”:

Then, choose the file you want to import, in this case the BPMNs that are stored within an .sgx file. Use the options as selected in the below screenshot and import the files. These are the BPMN Models that we use in the dashboards, you will need them for the next step.

Now, please set up the dashboards: Navigate into the pipeline again, and click on the process on the right side that you have created and attached to the pipeline:

  • Please import all the dashboards.
  • You can find the dashboards in the note.
  • Hint: Make sure that you are in the right tab. You want to be in the “Dashboards”-Tab within the process.
  • Then click import on the top-right and drag-and-drop all dashboards within this specific note.

If you now look through the imported dashboards, you might see some spots coming up where there is a process model missing or where you have to link an example diagram. As soon as you encounter this message: Please map the process model (that we have imported earlier in Step 7 into Process Manager) to the current dashboard. You can do this by clicking on the top-right three dots, into the settings. Here, you select the name of the process (e.g. Source to Pay) and get the fitting process model as a recommendation. Select the process model and it will appear in a preview as below. Then, click “Map activities”.

Next, map the specific events from the data to the events in the process model. You can do that manually, but also automatically where matching names get assigned to each other automatically (recommended way). You can do this by selecting “Auto-map” in the top-right as seen below, and confirm the pop-up.

Now that your process is mapped to the events in the data, you can return to your dashboard and the process will be properly set up.

Another type of diagram that need to be added is example diagrams. These diagrams provide process examples for specific use cases within a dashboard. If you encounter something like this within your dashboard you have to insert this specific diagram into the dashboard. You can do that by clicking on “Create Widget” within the dashboard, and select “Give it a try” when asked if you want to switch to the new widget creation experience (only by switching, you will be able to add the example diagrams).

Then, click “Edit” by clicking on the three dots in the respective widget for the example diagram, and this side panel opens on the right. In here, type the name of the diagram (as seen in the title of the widget, here e.g.: Example: Overdelivery case charged correct), and select the diagram that is suggested. This only works after the diagram has been uploaded into the process manager via the .sgx import that was described previously.

Congratulations, you are all set!

  • Now you might want to take a look into access rights for the processes and the dashboards that you have created. Bear in mind that at this point, only the person that set up everything has access. Check out our documentation on to manage access rights here

As you have extracted the data once while running the initial setup, you might want to schedule the data load automatically going forward. This way, the data in Process Insights is continuously refreshed. You can set the scheduled extraction by navigating into the source data, and click “Schedule” on the top-right. In the pop-up seen below, you can specify the schedule on which the data should be continuously extracted, provided that the data is available in SAP Signavio Process Insights and the connection to the ERP system is still up and running.

As mentioned earlier, with the new connector the process of refreshing data is becoming smoother and more focused on user needs: From now on, plug and gain users who have completed the initial set-up, can individually take-over the “plug”-phase at any desired time. In case you are already an active plug and gain user and want to leverage the new connector, make sure to reach out to your SAP contact to get started.

If you are not an active plug and gain user yet and want to learn more about it or request a demo, you can find out more here.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Hamburg #4
entry.content:
Liebe SAP Community Hamburg,
am 26.09.2024 ist es wieder soweit - der nächste SAP Stammtisch in Hamburg findet statt. Es wird rund um das Thema SAP Business AI gehen und welche Alternativen es für On-Premise Systeme geben könnte. Wir freuen uns auf einen guten Austausch in unserer Lieblingslocation an der Alster!⚓

Bitte meldet euch hier für die Veranstaltung an.

 Wir freuen uns auf euch!

entry.title: WhatsApp Chatbot for SAP Commerce application
entry.content:
With WhatsApp being an integral part of daily communication for many, integrating chatbots into this platform offers unparalleled convenience. Consumers can initiate conversations with businesses effortlessly, using a familiar interface they're comfortable with. Whether it's scheduling appointments, making reservations, checking order status, or ongoing/personalized promotions, the process becomes streamlined and accessible, catering to diverse preferences and lifestyles. It offers a treasure trove of benefits for both consumers and businesses

Cost-Efficient Operations

Implementing WhatsApp chatbots can significantly reduce operational costs for businesses. By automating routine inquiries and tasks, companies can optimize resource allocation and reallocate human agents to more complex issues requiring personalized attention. This not only improves efficiency but also minimizes the need for extensive manpower, resulting in substantial cost savings over time.

Data-Driven Insights

Chatbots are not just responders; they're data collectors and analyzers. By capturing user interactions and preferences, businesses can gain valuable insights into consumer behavior, preferences, and pain points. This data-driven approach empowers companies to refine their products, services, and marketing strategies, delivering more targeted and personalized experiences. Moreover, it enables predictive analytics, allowing businesses to anticipate customer needs and proactively address them.

Seamless Transactional Experiences

WhatsApp chatbots facilitate seamless transactions, enabling users to make purchases, payments, and bookings directly within the chat interface. Integrating payment gateways and e-commerce platforms simplifies the buying process, reducing friction and cart abandonment rates. Additionally, businesses can leverage chatbots to send order confirmations, shipping updates, and receipts, keeping customers informed at every stage of the transaction.

Scalability and Flexibility

One of the greatest advantages of WhatsApp chatbots is their scalability and flexibility. Whether catering to a handful of inquiries or managing thousands of interactions simultaneously, these chatbots can handle varying workloads effortlessly, ensuring consistent service quality irrespective of demand fluctuations. Moreover, they can be easily customized and updated to adapt to changing business requirements and market dynamics, providing agility and resilience in today's fast-paced environment.

Below is few interesting research result done by different marketing organization –

  • According to a recent study by ThriveMyWay, around 42% of B2C businesses have embraced WhatsApp chatbots.
  • In fact, if you set up a WhatsApp chatbot for your eCommerce business, you can significantly save on cost. Juniper’s research proved the same, mentioning that businesses can save $8 billion per year by integrating chatbots into their operations.
  • Cooby’s study found WhatsApp messages from brands have an open rate of around 70%.
  • McKinsey’s study also found that around 71% of customers want personalized interactions from brands.

Futuristic and evolving use cases-

  • Virtual Shopping Assistants - WhatsApp chatbots could evolve into virtual shopping assistants, providing personalized recommendations, assisting with product selection, and offering real-time support during the entire shopping journey. These assistants could utilize AI and machine learning algorithms to understand individual preferences, style choices, and purchase history to deliver tailored shopping experiences.
  • AI-Powered Customer Insights - Chatbots can gather valuable customer insights by analyzing interactions, sentiment, and preferences. In the future, they may incorporate advanced analytics and natural language processing to provide businesses with deeper insights into customer behavior, allowing for more targeted marketing campaigns, product development, and customer service enhancements.
  • Healthcare Support and Telemedicine - With the growing importance of telemedicine and remote healthcare services, WhatsApp chatbots could play a significant role in providing medical advice, scheduling appointments, and even monitoring patient health metrics. Integrating chatbots with healthcare databases and AI algorithms could enable more accurate symptom analysis and triage, improving access to healthcare services for patients worldwide.

Keeping in mind all the above objectives, we can integrate WhatsApp chat bot with SAP commerce application to significantly improves customer experiences and reduces communication barriers.
Below are few very common use cases in SAP commerce that can be integrated.
1. Order status check - User can start interaction to check their order status. Chatbot will ask for user email address and order number. Upon confirming those details bot will check the status for the given input and send it to user.
2. Get the list of personalized promotions - User can start interaction to get their personalized ongoing promotions. Chatbot will ask for user email address. Upon providing the email address bot will check for the eligible ongoing promotions and send those to the user.
3. Product Recommendation - Chatbot can tailor responses and suggest relevant products to users based on their preferences, behavior, and historical interactions with the platform.
4. Customer Support - Chatbot can handle common queries, provide product information, and guide users through processes.
5. FAQs and Self-Service - Chatbot can provide instant answers to frequently asked questions.

Technical Insight

For technical implementation we can leverage an AWS Lex Chatbot using a WhatsApp business profile to drive customer engagement. We will be using SAP commerce occ Rest webservices as webhook to respond to user queries. 

 

Conclusion

WhatsApp chatbots represent a paradigm shift in customer-business interactions, offering unparalleled convenience, efficiency, and engagement. By harnessing the power of artificial intelligence and natural language processing, businesses can elevate the customer experience, drive operational efficiency, and unlock new opportunities for growth. As technology continues to evolve, embracing WhatsApp chatbots isn't just an option, it's a strategic imperative for businesses seeking to thrive in the digital era.

entry.title: SAP Social Sabbatical – Reflections & Gratitude
entry.content:
Just before SAP Sapphire in Orlando this year, I returned from my SAP Social Sabbatical program in Kenya. I am sharing with you my take on this incredible program because I realize that many are not familiar with it, yet the program presents an amazing opportunity for all SAP catalysts and executives to drive positive change - both within our communities and for our personal and professional growth.

SAP Social Sabbatical is an award-winning portfolio of global volunteering consultancy programs that empower SAP employees to share their knowledge, skills, and expertise with non-profits and social enterprises all around the world. In its purest form, the social enterprise is a real, income-generating business, but one that has a clear social mission. It is a pioneering and burgeoning sector, numbering in the millions worldwide.
SAP is not a social enterprise, but we are inspired by them and our Social Sabbatical program is an opportunity to engage with these inspiring ventures and their visionary founders. It is also an opportunity to experience SAP's purpose - to help the world run better and improve people’s lives - on a very tacit and personal level.

To date, the program has positively impacted 7M+ lives, helped create 460+ jobs, and supported 531 organizations worldwide through 1,601 SAP employees, of which 47% have been our women colleagues. Within it, there are various global, regional, and local programs tailored for SAP executives and the talent in the Catalyst Development Program, so everyone can find an opportunity to volunteer for something suited to their skillset

Personally, I had stepped away from my day-to-day responsibilities for two weeks to participate in the Kenya program, but I came back with years of energy and positivity to give back to our business, our team, SAP and the communities we serve. That amount of motivation, drive and purpose, is the tangible outcome of our Social Sabbatical program I would like all of you to experience.

I met very young people who already know their potential, and who are incredibly capable. They are executing very strategic world-class programs and leveraging technology and AI as much as some of the most capable in our teams in SAP today. But, while globalization and technology have made education more accessible to all over the last couple of decades, they couldn’t offer access to equal local and global opportunities at the same pace. SAP stepped up to help through the best we have to offer – access to markets, our technology and our incredible and skilled talent.

I supported a social enterprise called Taka Taka Ni Mali, founded by one of Kenya’s most successful women entrepreneurs who was named the Woman Entrepreneur of the Year in Kenya in 2018. The name of her enterprise in Swahili means ‘Waste is Wealth’ as the business focuses on managing and recycling waste, with a vision to turn Kenya into an environmentally sustainable nation. The business relies on donors to fund their enterprise, but Mary, the founder, is intent on making Taka Taka Ni Mali a financially independent enterprise with sustainable long-term success.

We were tasked with helping the business explore monetization opportunities to self-fund its social mission. Two weeks later, two of my SAP colleagues and I, representing three different SAP Board areas, redesigned Taka Taka Ni Mali’s entire business model, identifying five key monetization opportunities, and building the financial model and business plan for two of them. The plan was adopted, and the execution started while we were still in Nairobi!
These two weeks had a massive impact on my personal growth too.

Being taken out of my comfort zone - from the daily hum and drum of the business world - and being thrown into a problem that affected the lives of the people in front of me, brought a new level of clarity, efficiency and personal motivation to resolve that problem! The importance of finding a personal connection between the problem and my motivation to solve it, is one of the key lessons from this experience for me.

I was also successfully reminded about the power of conviction – seeing the determination and the absence of fear to go after one’s conviction even in such challenging environments, reignited my personal conviction in the journey we have charted for our Digital motion. I come back stoked, knowing that a few determined individuals can indeed move mountains. We, on the other hand, are a +1,000 men and woman-strong team in the Customer Success Digital motion – our goals need to remain incredibly ambitious - because we CAN attain them.

SAP is setting an incredibly high bar in the industry with this program and I encourage you all to check if you are eligible to participate at least once in your career. Programs like these help us align our actions with the values we stand for and the culture we strive to uphold - where empathy and kindness can be, and are, executed in harmony with our grit and high performance.

I’ve always been proud to be an SAPer, but I’ve never been as proud as I am now!

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Plus-Pune (August 2024)
entry.content:
Greetings SAP Community in Pune & Mumbai! We have some exciting news to share with you!


We are thrilled to announce that the SAP Community events will be making a comeback in Pune & Mumbai, starting with the SAP Stammtisch Plus on August 30th, 2024.

This event is an excellent opportunity for SAP professionals, enthusiasts, and anyone interested in learning more about SAP technologies to come together and learn, network with like-minded individuals, and have fun.

Are you wondering what an SAP Stammtisch Plus event is?

SAP Stammtisch Plus is a local grassroots community event where SAP Community members come together to share knowledge about SAP-related topics. The event's primary focus is networking & discussions, with 2 or 3 sessions from community members.

Learn more about it at: SAP Stammtisch - what is it about?

What is in it for you?

Let’s cut to the chase, what will you get by attending or contributing to this event?

1. Networking – You get to meet like-minded tech folks and have more space for networking.
2. Learning     – You will learn about the latest SAP technologies from experts.


 

 

 

 

When?
30th August 2024 from 2:00 PM till 4:00 PM. Registration starts at 1:30 PM.

Where?
3rd Floor, Wing 2 Block 1, International Tech Park, Wagholi Village, Kharadi Road, Pune - 411014. Phone: + 91 20 41985101

Registration

Please register using the below link. - SAP Stammtisch Plus (konfhub.com)

Note: We have only limited seats available, so register ONLY if you plan to attend IN-PERSON on August 30, Friday in Pune.

Organizers & Volunteers:

  • @Pari1 
  • @Asutosh21 
  • @Shweta_Kadam 
  • @Nitin_Rakh 
  • @SmitaP1 
  • @ranjeetkumar07 
  • @Devkambli 


We hope to see you all there and look forward to an engaging and productive event. Don't forget to register using the link provided above to secure your spot. See you soon!

Best Regards
SAP Community Group – Pune

entry.title: Experience at First SAP Community Meet-Up || Powered By Innoverv || 17th August,2024 || Kolkata
entry.content:
Greetings, Everyone!!

We as a SAP Community Kolkata team wanted to take a moment to express our heartfelt gratitude for arranging the exceptional event, SAP Community meet-up powered by INNOVERV on their first year anniversary and visionary expansion of 150+ more SAP consultants based out of Kolkata.

Also thank you @sveabecker , @laurap,  @ChristopherA,  @Katherina  for giving us the opportunity to grow the SAP Kolkata Community. Also, thank you @DataTherapist for making us more capable to organize and handle such events. Thank you @SitabjaB, @Soham_SCM, @SohamBhKoL, @Shrestha, @TanushreeD, @Kamallica, @soumyajit, @Victor  and Innoverv Volunteers for being a great team.

 

Key Insights of today’s event

  1. How Gen-AI and Cloud is impacting the global market.

Gen-AI within SAP's cloud environment accelerates innovation by enabling advanced analytics, predictive modelling, and customized solutions, helping businesses quickly adapt to market changes. SAP's cloud infrastructure provides scalable and flexible resources, allowing companies to expand and innovate without the constraints of traditional IT setups. With AI-driven insights and analytics, SAP helps businesses make data-driven decisions, leading to better strategic planning and market positioning. SAP's cloud solutions facilitate global collaboration by offering remote access to data and applications, which enhances cross-border teamwork and operational agility.

  1. Importance of SAP Sustainability.

SAP’s solutions help companies track and reduce their carbon footprint, implement sustainable practices, and achieve their environmental goals, which aligns with global sustainability standards. SAP provides tools for better reporting and monitoring of environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics, enabling businesses to demonstrate their commitment to sustainability to stakeholders and regulators. SAP’s focus on sustainability fosters innovation in areas such as resource efficiency and circular economy practices, leading to new business models and technologies that support long-term environmental goals. SAP’s sustainability tools assist companies in meeting increasingly stringent environmental regulations and standards, ensuring compliance and mitigating regulatory risks.

  1. SAP Product Roadmap.

Core Areas of Focus - Expansion and enhancement of SAP Cloud Platform and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to support scalability, flexibility, and integration across various cloud environments. Integration of AI, machine learning, and advanced analytics into SAP S/4HANA and other core ERP solutions to enable data-driven decision-making and automation. Continuous improvement of SAP Fiori and user interface design to enhance usability, personalization, and accessibility for end-users.

Innovation and Technology - Integration of Gen-AI capabilities to automate processes, enhance predictive analytics, and generate insights for more informed business strategies. Tools and features to support sustainability goals, such as carbon footprint tracking, resource management, and compliance with environmental regulations. Development of more seamless integration capabilities with third-party applications and technologies to ensure a connected and cohesive IT ecosystem.

Product Releases and Updates - Regular updates to core products like SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, and others, including new features, enhancements, and security improvements. Introduction of next-generation tools and platforms that leverage emerging technologies to address evolving business needs.

Customer-Centric Enhancements - Incorporation of customer feedback into product development to ensure solutions meet real-world needs and deliver value. Expanded resources for customer support, training, and adoption to help users effectively leverage new features and updates.

Strategic Partnerships and Ecosystem - Building strategic partnerships with other technology providers and industry leaders to enhance SAP’s offerings and integrate with complementary solutions. Growth of the SAP App Centre and other marketplaces to offer a broader range of third-party applications and add-ons.

  1. Market dependency and demand optimisation using AI-ML, Analytics and Cloud.
  2. Work for Each Other, Work with Each Other.
  3. Work under pressure and tackle difficult scenarios.

The Panel discussion about how Kolkata is becoming the Silicon Valley of SAP and the SAP growing community in world-wide, the optimal futuristic features i.e. Sustainability, SAP BTP, GEN-AI and Cloud with our renowned panelist ( Partha Chakraborty, Ambarish Dasgupta, Anindya Mitra, Asidhara Lahiri, Sourav Ganguly ). Also, thanking to ( Jo Whalley ) for guiding us the journey of growing SAP and INNOVERV disruption. We greatly appreciate ( Sana Ganguly and Veena )  for hosting this amazing event to build-up more networks.

Thank you for your unwavering support to grow the Kolkata community and for making this journey both challenging and rewarding. Together, we are not only achieving our goals but setting new standards for excellence.

Here are some beautiful moments and memories for today’s events. We are looking forward to grow more.

#SITKOL #SAPCOMMUNITYKOLKATA

entry.title: Utilize Requirement Modeling in Software Engineering for Functional Specification [FS]
entry.content:
Introduction

The functional specification is very crucial for requirement documentation and management when all the functional and technical SAP consultants work on SAP RICEFW during the realization of any SAP project.

In this blog post I would like to discuss about requirements engineering standard practices and requirement modeling in high level which can be utilized for Functional Specification derived from requirements documentation.

Requirements Engineering:

  • is a set of procedures used to establish the customer requirements from a system including the constraints under which it operates and is developed.
  • is a well organized approach to eliciting, organizing, and documenting the requirements of the system, and a set of procedures that formulates and maintains agreement between the customer and the project team on the changing requirements of the system.

Requirements are:

  • Statements of needs from a expected system.
  • System features and constraints characterization that are identified while the requirements elicitation.

Requirements Activities:

Following diagram depicted activities of requirement engineering process in a high level.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Requirements Types:

Requirements can be classified under following ways.

  • Functional requirements
    • Basic subject matter how behave the system and are measured by concrete means such as data values, logic for decision making and algorithms.
  • Non-functional requirements
    • Behavioral properties that the required functions must have, such as performance and scalability, reliability, etc.
  • Project constraints
    • Recognize the ways of final product must suitable into the world.
    • Integration with some existing hardware, software or business practice.
    • Defined project scope.
  • Project drivers
    • Business- related forces.
    • Purpose of the product is a project driver, as are all of the stakeholders.
  • Project issues
    • Define the conditions under which the project will be done.
    • Included to present a coherent picture of all factors contributing to the success or failure.

IEEE 830 Documentation Standard for a Software Requirements Specification (1998):

Criteria for a high-quality requirements set are:

  • Unambiguous: Every statement has unique interpretation. Terms should be clear and well defined.
  • Complete: All major requirements are considered. No items have been disregarded for future definition.
  • Verifiable: All functionalities defined as part of the project have a corresponding test to determine whether they have been successfully delivered.
  • Consistent: Final product's end users benefit by getting a product that is easy to use enhancing the user experience.
  • Modifiable: The cost of change and refers to the ease with which a software system can accommodate changes.
  • Traceable: Uniquely identification is required for each referenced requirement.
  • Correct: Every stated requirement represents something required of the system to be built.

Requirements Modelling:

Unified Modeling Language (UML)  and Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN) can be used for requirement modeling with graphical representation by diagrams. You can freely use draw.io online application to prepare diagrams.

  • Functional Requirements
    • Hierarchy
    • Data Flow Diagrams
    • Use Cases
    • Activity Diagrams
    • Other graphical models
  • Data Requirements
    • Entity relationship diagram
  • Linking data and functional requirements
  • Maintaining the link between textual and graphical requirements documents

Benefits of Models:

  • Provide a visual, non-technical representation
  • Are easy to understand
  • Quick to produce and easy to amend
  • Use limited number of symbols with specific meanings
    • implicit presentation of information
    • concise system descriptions
    • enable code generation through CASE
  • Minimize redundancy of information
    • effects of changes are easily identified and incorporated
  • Use a simple, top-down expansion
    • easy progression between levels of detail
    • convenient review of the overall system structure

Good Requirements Guidelines:

  • Add simple direct sentences
    • As short as possible but no shorter
  • Add a limited vocabulary
    • Project glossary also recommend
  • Focus on results (goal)
  • Define verifiable criteria
    • later relate to Acceptance Criterion

In conclusion, with above blog post your are able to get an overview idea of requirement engineering paradigm and possible opportunities to utilize them with sap RICEFW requirement modeling.

Hope you have gained something it and stay tuned for real world scenario with simple examples in my future blog.

entry.title: More Enhancements for SAP Community
entry.content:
Although it's been more than a month since our last announcement about enhancements (meaning improvements and bug fixes), I'm happy to report that we recently rolled out some fairly significant updates to the platform -- including perhaps the thing most demanded by members. Specifically…

Additional Sorting Options for SAP Managed Tags

Since the migration to the new platform, members have been asking for a way to sort SAP Managed Tag feeds by content type (e.g., blog posts vs. Q&A). Now members can do that and more -- sorting by all types of posts available within SAP Community, as well as by creation date, activity (e.g., most commented), and area within the platform (e.g., a specific Q&A board).

See for yourself. Visit any SAP Managed Tag page and try out the various options until you're able to find the content that most appeals to you.

More Prominent Placement of SAP Managed Tags

Another adjustment related to SAP Managed Tags: They now appear at the top of a post (e.g., question, blog), so readers can identify immediately what the content is about.

In the previous layout, the tags appeared at the end of the post, and members commented that they'd like this information sooner. Now they get it right away -- at the beginning of the post.

Better Log-In Experience

If you are one of the members who complained about the constant need to log in each and every time you visited us here, then you may have already noticed this change.

With this improvement, when you log in, you stay logged in for 30 days -- as long as you don't log out/clear your cache.

This improvement goes across platforms, so if you log in to SAP.com, support.sap.com, or learning.sap.com, you'll log in automatically when navigating to SAP Community. The same applies if you log in to SAP Community before going to SAP.com.

Group Curators Easier to Identify

If you visit a lot of areas in SAP Community, you may have thought that my colleagues and I ran just about every group -- because our names often appeared next to "Owned by" on every landing page.

In actuality, due to the way that the platform was set up, the person who created the group would appear as its primary owner, and since group creation is one of my team's responsibilities, my colleagues and I would show up as group owners -- but it's really the curators who oversee the groups once we set them up.

This approach made things a bit confusing for visitors who wanted to connect with the members who were actually managing a group. Not surprisingly, they'd end up contacting my colleagues and me.

Now each group shows its curators as the owners, so visitors can easily identify the members who kindly volunteer to run groups for specific locations, interests, and so on.  (I mean, sure, I'd love to talk to you about the awesome Melbourne group, but you're better off reaching out to my friend @pjcools, who is doing the work there. So it's better you see his name as the group owner now instead of mine!)

Clearer Options for Blog Boards

As explained in this post, our FAQ, the how-to videos, and our official SAP Community tutorial, bloggers who want to share content in the Products and Technology areas need to select between either "by Members" or "by SAP" boards (depending on whether they work for SAP). Still, it was not uncommon for bloggers to select the wrong board -- only to be greeted by this error message as a result:

Now, to avoid that experience, the Create a Blog button will be grayed out when bloggers attempt to post content in a board that isn't open to their submissions. For example, as an SAP employee, I'll see this if I try to blog in the CRM and CX Blogs by Members board (because I should be posting in the CRM and CX Blogs by SAP board instead):

With this change, we hope our bloggers will be able to locate the correct boards more easily when they wish to share their knowledge with other community members.

Bullet Bug Be Gone!

I'll wrap up with a fix for something that had been bugging members.

Members had reported that when they added a nested bullet list within a bullet list, the system would automatically add an extra space. That's been fixed:

While that fix may not seem like a major thing, I know this bug had been aggravating people during the editing process, so I'm glad to pass along this news.

I'll be back later this week with an SAP Community Voice newsletter highlighting new and popular content. And as more improvements and fixes get introduced, I'll be reporting them here in What's New as well. So remember to subscribe to this board and keep an eye out for the latest announcements.
